cues.title: Watch the motion
cues.text: Shh—look closely. Two skaters are still. Watch their hands. They press, then both platforms roll apart. Freeze the picture. Point to the pair. Where did the pushes act?

cues.title: A force needs two
cues.text: A force is a push or pull during an interaction. Skater A pushes on B. At once, B pushes on A. One interaction gives two forces, acting on different objects. Watch the contact again.

cues.title: Name both objects
cues.text: Read the labels. A on B belongs on B, because B receives that push. B on A belongs on A. The arrows are equal and point opposite ways. Two objects. Two forces.

cues.title: Motion is evidence
cues.text: Now compare the evidence. Before contact: still. After contact: moving apart. What changed? Their motion. We cannot see a force floating by itself, but we can observe what the interaction changed.

cues.title: The push has ended
cues.text: Ooh, careful—many scientists think the push rides inside the skater. Let’s check. Hands touch: contact arrows appear. Hands separate: the arrows vanish. On this low-friction track, the skaters keep gliding while other forces still act.

cues.title: Push or pull
cues.text: Contact forces can push or pull. A hand pushes a cart away. A rope pulls a trolley closer. Name each pair: hand on cart; rope on trolley. One object alone is not a complete explanation.

cues.title: No touch required
cues.text: Keep your eye on the gap. These magnets never touch, yet both carts move. That is non-contact evidence. Earth also pulls a falling book without touching it. Again: which two objects?

cues.title: Contact or non-contact
cues.text: Quick check. Which two objects interact? Are they touching? Foot on ball: contact. Magnet on magnet: non-contact. Earth on book: non-contact. Strength does not choose the label; touching does.

cues.title: Test one change
cues.text: Let’s test two pushes fairly. Same carts. Same floor. Same starting line. We change only the push, then measure distance. Change several things, and the evidence cannot tell us what mattered.

cues.title: Repair the explanation
cues.text: A tempting idea: the ball keeps moving, so the kick must be inside it. Let’s check. Foot touches ball: the contact push acts. Foot leaves: that push ends. The ball keeps moving. Interaction first; changed motion afterwards.

cues.title: Predict the evidence
cues.text: Final prediction. Two magnet carts start still with a gap, then move apart. Which observations support a non-contact push? Hold your answer. The gap stays visible, both motions change, and no hand touches either cart.

cues.title: Your physics move
cues.text: You have the physicist’s move. Name the pair. Decide: touch or no touch. Use a change in motion as evidence. A force is not hidden stuff inside an object. It is an interaction.
